pi_items.source: Task description states 'change from 3 (Medium) to 2 (Low)'; verifier.py line 50 defines TARGET_APPETITE='2'; setup_eramba.sh line 138 sets initial risk_appetite=3; export_result.sh retrieves final values
setup_eramba.sh lines 138-140 UPDATE risk_appetites for model 'Risks'; task description maps to 'Enterprise Risks'; Eramba documentation confirms three risk types including Asset/Enterprise Risks
setup_eramba.sh lines 138-140 UPDATE risk_appetites for model 'ThirdPartyRisks'; task description maps to 'Third Party Risks'; Eramba documentation confirms Third-Party risk type

setup_eramba.sh lines 138-140 UPDATE risk_appetites for model 'BusinessContinuities'; task description maps to 'Business Continuity Risks'; Eramba documentation confirms Business Continuity Plans module with risk integration
docker-compose.yml line 40 maps port '8080:80', line 51 sets PUBLIC_ADDRESS='http://localhost:8080'; setup_task.sh line 27 navigates to 'http://localhost:8080/dashboard/dashboard'

privileged_info_summary: Eramba is a real open-source GRC (Governance, Risk & Compliance) platform. The task configures risk appetite for three legitimate Eramba risk domains: Risks (Enterprise Risks), ThirdPartyRisks (Third Party Risks), and BusinessContinuities (Business Continuity Risks). The target risk appetite level is 2 (Low), requiring changes from the initial level 3 (Medium). The Eramba instance is deployed via Docker on localhost:8080 (HTTP). The risk configuration data is synthetically generated by the setup_task.sh script, which resets risk_appetite values to 3 and method values to 0 (Simple Threshold mode) before each task run. The verifier.py checks that all three models have appetite=2, method=0, and calc_method='eramba', with no unauthorized method changes allowed.
